title: Roblox Earnings Report: Key Takeaways
text: Roblox Corporation was scheduled to report fourth-quarter and full-year 2021 results after market close on February 15, 2022, with an analyst conference call the following day at 8:30 a.m. ET. Wall Street anticipated fourth-quarter revenue of $767.6 million, representing approximately 147% year-over-year growth compared to Q4 2020's $310 million. Adjusted earnings (loss) per share were projected at negative $0.14, indicating analysts expected the loss to widen by 27% compared to the prior year's negative $0.11 per share. Analysts emphasized tracking several key indicators including Average daily active users (DAUs), Hours engaged on the platform, Revenue bookings growth, and Average bookings per DAU. In Q3 2021, the company reported 47.3 million DAUs with 28% bookings growth, though bookings per user declined 2%, attributed to geographic user base shifts toward Asia Pacific. The article notes that technology stocks faced headwinds due to anticipated Federal Reserve interest rate hikes, meaning Roblox would need to significantly exceed expectations to maintain stock momentum.
